name: Curcumin
health_risks: None known; natural turmeric derivative

name: Riboflavin (Vitamin B2)
health_risks: None; essential vitamin

name: Tartrazine
health_risks: Hyperactivity in children, allergic reactions, asthma worsening

name: Quinoline Yellow
health_risks: Hyperactivity in children; banned in USA and Australia

name: Yellow 2G
health_risks: Hyperactivity; banned in many countries

name: Sunset Yellow FCF
health_risks: Hyperactivity in children, linked to ADHD, possible carcinogen

name: Carmines (Cochineal)
health_risks: Allergic reactions; unsuitable for vegetarians/vegans

name: Azorubine (Carmoisine)
health_risks: Hyperactivity in children, asthma, allergic reactions

name: Ponceau 4R
health_risks: Hyperactivity; banned in USA and Norway

name: Erythrosine
health_risks: Thyroid disruption; banned in cosmetics

name: Allura Red AC
health_risks: Hyperactivity in children, possible carcinogen

name: Indigotine (Indigo Carmine)
health_risks: Nausea and vomiting at high doses

name: Brilliant Blue FCF
health_risks: May cause chromosomal damage at high doses

name: Green S
health_risks: Hyperactivity; banned in USA, Australia, Japan

name: Caramel I (plain)
health_risks: None known

name: Caramel IV (sulphite-ammonia)
health_risks: Contains 4-MEI, possible carcinogen in large quantities; common in colas

name: Beta-Carotene
health_risks: None; precursor to Vitamin A

name: Titanium Dioxide
health_risks: Possible carcinogen (IARC Group 2B); banned as food additive in EU

name: Sorbic Acid
health_risks: Mild skin irritant; generally recognised as safe

name: Potassium Sorbate
health_risks: May cause DNA damage in lymphocytes at high doses

name: Benzoic Acid
health_risks: Asthma trigger; reacts with Vitamin C to form benzene

name: Sodium Benzoate
health_risks: Hyperactivity in children; forms carcinogenic benzene with ascorbic acid; ADHD link

name: Potassium Benzoate
health_risks: Same as sodium benzoate; benzene formation risk

name: Sulphur Dioxide
health_risks: Asthma attacks, respiratory irritation; allergic reactions in sulfite-sensitive individuals

name: Sodium Sulphite
health_risks: Respiratory problems in asthmatics

name: Sodium Metabisulphite
health_risks: Asthma, allergic reactions, respiratory irritation

name: Potassium Nitrite
health_risks: Forms nitrosamines (carcinogenic); dangerous for infants

name: Sodium Nitrite
health_risks: Carcinogenic nitrosamines; particularly dangerous for infants and in processed meats

name: Sodium Nitrate
health_risks: Converted to nitrite in body; carcinogenic nitrosamines

name: Lactic Acid
health_risks: None; naturally occurring in fermented foods

name: Propionic Acid
health_risks: May cause migraines at high doses; linked to autism markers in some animal studies

name: Sodium Propanoate
health_risks: Migraine trigger; used in breads

name: Malic Acid
health_risks: None known; naturally in apples

name: Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C)
health_risks: None; essential vitamin

name: Tocopherol-rich Extract (Vitamin E)
health_risks: None at food levels

name: TBHQ (Tertiary Butylhydroquinone)
health_risks: ADHD, restlessness, possible carcinogen at high doses; immune system disruption

name: BHA (Butylated Hydroxyanisole)
health_risks: Possible carcinogen (IARC Group 2B); endocrine disruptor

name: BHT (Butylated Hydroxytoluene)
health_risks: Liver and kidney damage at high doses; possible carcinogen; endocrine disruptor

name: Lecithin
health_risks: None known; soy-derived versions may trigger soy allergy

name: Citric Acid
health_risks: Tooth enamel erosion in excess; generally safe

name: Sodium Citrates
health_risks: None; used in sports drinks and processed cheese

name: Phosphoric Acid
health_risks: Bone density loss with excessive consumption; tooth enamel erosion; common in colas

name: Potassium Phosphates
health_risks: Excessive phosphate intake linked to kidney disease and bone loss

name: Carrageenan
health_risks: Gut inflammation, IBS aggravation, possible carcinogen (degraded form)

name: Locust Bean Gum (Carob Bean Gum)
health_risks: None known; natural fibre

name: Guar Gum
health_risks: None; may cause bloating at high doses

name: Xanthan Gum
health_risks: None known at typical food levels

name: Sorbitol
health_risks: Laxative effect in excess (>50g/day); gas and bloating

name: Mannitol
health_risks: Laxative effect; digestive upset

name: Polysorbate 80 (Tween 80)
health_risks: Gut microbiome disruption, inflammatory bowel disease, metabolic syndrome

name: Triphosphates
health_risks: High phosphate load; kidney strain

name: Polyphosphates
health_risks: Excessive phosphate; kidney disease risk

name: Microcrystalline Cellulose
health_risks: None; inert filler

name: Carboxymethyl Cellulose (CMC)
health_risks: Gut microbiome disruption in animal studies; promotes gut inflammation

name: Mono- and Diglycerides of Fatty Acids
health_risks: May contain trans fats; contributes to cardiovascular risk

name: Polyglycerol Polyricinoleate (PGPR)
health_risks: Liver and kidney effects in rodent studies at high doses

name: Sodium Carbonates
health_risks: None at typical levels

name: Ammonium Carbonates
health_risks: None; decomposes completely on baking

name: Potassium Chloride
health_risks: None at food levels; cardiac risk only at very high doses

name: Calcium Chloride
health_risks: None known; used in paneer and tofu making

name: Silicon Dioxide (Silica)
health_risks: Inhalation risk (manufacturing); safe as food additive

name: Magnesium Silicate (Talc)
health_risks: Possible carcinogen if contaminated with asbestos fibres

name: Fatty Acids
health_risks: None known

name: Glucono Delta Lactone (GDL)
health_risks: None known; naturally in honey and fruit juice

name: Monosodium Glutamate (MSG)
health_risks: Chinese Restaurant Syndrome (headache, flushing) in sensitive individuals; high sodium content; neurotoxic at very high doses

name: Disodium Guanylate
health_risks: Should be avoided by gout patients (purine-derived)

name: Disodium Inosinate
health_risks: Gout risk; often used alongside MSG to amplify umami

name: Disodium Ribonucleotides
health_risks: Gout; skin reactions; amplifies MSG effects

name: L-Leucine
health_risks: None; essential amino acid

name: Dimethyl Polysiloxane (PDMS)
health_risks: None known at food levels

name: Beeswax
health_risks: None; not vegan

name: L-Cysteine
health_risks: Often derived from duck feathers or pig bristles; not vegetarian/kosher

name: Potassium Bromate
health_risks: Proven carcinogen; banned in EU, India, Canada, and most countries

name: Benzoyl Peroxide
health_risks: Bleaching agent; destroys nutrients in flour

name: Acesulfame Potassium (Acesulfame K)
health_risks: Possible effects on gut microbiome; methylene chloride (carcinogen) used in manufacture

name: Aspartame
health_risks: Headaches, depression, seizures (reports); IARC Group 2B possible carcinogen (2023); dangerous for phenylketonuria (PKU) patients

name: Cyclamates
health_risks: Possible carcinogen; banned in USA and India

name: Saccharin
health_risks: Bladder cancer risk in rodents; gut microbiome disruption; insulin resistance

name: Sucralose
health_risks: Gut microbiome disruption; elevated blood glucose at high doses in some studies

name: Steviol Glycosides (Stevia)
health_risks: None known at permitted levels; natural plant extract

name: Maltitol
health_risks: Laxative effect; raises blood sugar (not truly diabetic-safe)

name: Xylitol
health_risks: Laxative effect; extremely toxic to dogs

name: Tertiary Butylhydroquinone
health_risks: Immune disruption, ADHD, restlessness; possible carcinogen; found in instant noodles and fried snacks

name: Monosodium Glutamate
health_risks: Headache, flushing, sweating in sensitive individuals; high sodium

name: High Fructose Corn Syrup
health_risks: Obesity, fatty liver disease, insulin resistance, heart disease; metabolised differently from glucose

name: Maltodextrin
health_risks: High glycaemic index (GI>100); spikes blood sugar rapidly; gut microbiome disruption

name: Vanillin (Synthetic)
health_risks: Synthetic petrochemical derivative; possible headaches; not same as natural vanilla

name: Refined Wheat Flour (Maida)
health_risks: High glycaemic index; stripped of fibre and nutrients; spikes blood sugar

name: Partially Hydrogenated Vegetable Oil (Trans Fat)
health_risks: Heart disease, LDL increase, HDL reduction; strongly linked to cardiovascular mortality

name: Palm Oil
health_risks: High in saturated fat; linked to heart disease at high consumption; environmental concerns

name: Sulphites (general)
health_risks: Asthma attacks; severe allergic reactions; required to be labelled

name: Acetylated Distarch Adipate
health_risks: Ultra-processed; minimal direct harm but marker of highly processed food

name: Hydroxy Propyl Distarch Phosphate
health_risks: Ultra-processed marker; no direct toxicity proven

name: Starch Sodium Octenyl Succinate
health_risks: Ultra-processed marker; minimal direct harm

name: Carmine (Cochineal Extract)
health_risks: Allergic reactions; anaphylaxis in rare cases; not vegetarian (insect-derived)

name: Annatto (Bixin / Norbixin)
health_risks: ADHD in some children; IBS symptoms; allergic reactions

name: Acesulfame K
health_risks: Gut microbiome disruption; possible carcinogen (methylene chloride)

name: Aspartame
health_risks: IARC Group 2B carcinogen (2023); dangerous for PKU patients; headaches, depression

name: Annatto Extracts (Bixin, Norbixin)
health_risks: ADHD, IBS; mild allergen

name: Anthocyanins
health_risks: None; natural plant pigment with antioxidant properties

name: Calcium Sulphate
health_risks: None at food levels; occurs naturally

name: Sodium Aluminium Phosphate
health_risks: Aluminium accumulation linked to Alzheimer's disease; avoid in large amounts

name: Sodium Aluminosilicate
health_risks: Aluminium toxicity concern at prolonged high exposure

name: Polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP)
health_risks: May accumulate in body; used in dietary supplements and food supplements

name: Calcium Disodium EDTA
health_risks: Mineral depletion with excessive intake; chelates zinc and iron

name: Sodium Alginate
health_risks: None; derived from seaweed

name: Propylene Glycol
health_risks: Hives and eczema in sensitive individuals; can cause CNS depression at very high doses
